name: Va Hospital
description_short: VA Hospital Federal Credit Union is a healthcare-focused credit union established in 1951, serving over 1,700 members from its Little Rock, Arkansas headquarters with $8.8M in assets.
description_long:
VA Hospital Federal Credit Union was established in 1951 and is headquartered in Little Rock, Arkansas, specializing in financial services for healthcare industry professionals and their families. The credit union has grown to serve over 1,700 loyal members and manages approximately $8.8 million in assets across a single branch location with 2 ATMs. The institution maintains a stable, experienced team of over 4 dedicated employees as of 2026.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of personal banking and lending services including checking accounts, savings accounts, share draft accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans, personal loans, and IRA/KEOGH accounts. Members can access digital banking features including online banking, a mobile app for iOS and Android, bill pay, online loan payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and financial calculators. Wire transfer services and financial counseling are also available to support member needs.

VA Hospital FCU distinguishes itself through its specialization in serving healthcare professionals and demonstrated commitment to personalized customer service. Member reviews highlight exceptionally stable staffing with minimal turnover, and specific staff members (Debbie, Marty, Billie, and Andrew) are recognized by name for stellar customer service. The credit union actively involves members in product development, such as soliciting feedback during online banking rollouts. The institution has earned a 5.0-star rating based on member reviews, with members praising proactive fraud monitoring and responsive problem resolution.

The primary limitation is operational: the lobby is currently closed, and the institution may be experiencing service issues with its website and app. As a small, single-branch credit union, access is limited to the main office location in Little Rock with only 2 ATMs. The restrictive membership criteria (focused on healthcare industry workers) means the credit union is not available to the general public. While the $8.8M asset base provides stability, it is significantly smaller than major banking institutions, which may limit product variety and technological capabilities. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Bill pay and online loan payments
pros: Specialized focus on healthcare industry professionals with tailored financial services
Exceptionally stable staffing with minimal turnover, creating continuity of service relationships
5.0-star member rating with documented proactive fraud monitoring and rapid response to account issues
Member-centric approach including involvement in product development decisions (e.g., online banking features)
Comprehensive digital banking services including mobile app, bill pay, remote deposit, and e-statements
Low-cost structure typical of credit unions with personalized financial counseling included
Established 73-year history (since 1951) providing institutional stability and trust
cons: Lobby currently closed, limiting in-person service access
Only 1 branch and 2 ATMs available, restricting physical access for members
Website and app experiencing reported service issues with no clear resolution timeline
Restricted membership eligibility (healthcare industry focus only) prevents general public access
Small asset base ($8.8M) limits product variety and technological capabilities compared to larger institutions
best_for: Healthcare industry professionals (doctors, nurses, hospital staff) seeking personalized banking
VA Hospital employees and their families with established relationships at the institution
Healthcare workers in the Little Rock, Arkansas area wanting localized community banking
Individuals prioritizing relationship banking and staff continuity over digital convenience
diagnosis: VA Hospital Federal Credit Union is best for healthcare professionals in the Little Rock area seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with exceptional customer service and community credit union benefits. The primary caveat is that operational challenges (closed lobby, service issues) and limited physical access (1 branch, 2 ATMs) make it unsuitable for members requiring frequent in-person banking or robust digital-first services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
